---
title: "StackModelPredictions"
author: "Daniel Myung"
date: "2026-04-13"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r, cache= TRUE}
# --- Level 1 Meta-Model Evaluation ---
library(caret)
library(C50)
library(randomForest)
set.seed(12345)

# 1. Load the 30% Stacked Test Partition
# This is the 'holdout' data containing expert probabilities the meta-model hasn't seen
stack_test <- readRDS("depression_stack_test.rds")

# 2. Load the Level 1 Meta-Models
m_meta_tree <- readRDS("depression_meta_model_tree.rds")
m_meta_rf    <- readRDS("depression_meta_model_rf.rds")

# Ensure the actual response is a factor for the Confusion Matrix
# We use 'actual' which we defined in the stacking step
actual <- as.factor(stack_test$actual)

# 3. Generate Meta-Predictions & Confusion Matrices
# Tree Meta-Model (Uses the 4:1 Cost Matrix logic)
pred_tree <- predict(m_meta_tree, stack_test)
cm_tree   <- confusionMatrix(pred_tree, actual, positive = "1")

# Random Forest Meta-Model (Ensemble of Experts)
pred_rf <- predict(m_meta_rf, stack_test)
cm_rf   <- confusionMatrix(pred_rf, actual, positive = "1")

# 4. Display Results for Comparison
cat("--- LEVEL 1: C5.0 META-TREE (Depression) ---\n")
print(cm_tree$table)
cat("Kappa:", round(cm_tree$overall["Kappa"], 4), "\n")
cat("Sensitivity (Mental Illness):", round(cm_tree$byClass["Sensitivity"], 4), "\n\n")

cat("--- LEVEL 1: RANDOM FOREST META-MODEL (Depression) ---\n")
print(cm_rf$table)
cat("Kappa:", round(cm_rf$overall["Kappa"], 4), "\n")
cat("Sensitivity (Mental Illness):", round(cm_rf$byClass["Sensitivity"], 4), "\n")

# 5. Save Final Metrics Table
# This will be the centerpiece of your final report with Mike
stacked_metrics <- data.frame(
  Model    = c("STACKED_Tree", "STACKED_RF"),
  Accuracy = c(cm_tree$overall["Accuracy"], cm_rf$overall["Accuracy"]),
  Kappa    = c(cm_tree$overall["Kappa"], cm_rf$overall["Kappa"]),
  Sens     = c(cm_tree$byClass["Sensitivity"], cm_rf$byClass["Sensitivity"]),
  Spec     = c(cm_tree$byClass["Specificity"], cm_rf$byClass["Specificity"])
)

saveRDS(stacked_metrics, "depression_stacked_metrics.rds")
print(stacked_metrics)
```

```{r}
library(caret)
library(kernlab) # For SVM
library(C50)     # For Tree
library(nnet)    # For ANN
library(class)   # For KNN
library(randomForest)

# 1. LOAD THE MODELS
m_glm  <- readRDS("glm_model.rds")
m_knn  <- readRDS("knn_model_object.rds")
m_ann  <- readRDS("ann.rds")
m_svm  <- readRDS("svm_rbf.rds")
m_tree <- readRDS("c50_model.rds")

# 2. LOAD THE TEST DATA
# 'test_raw' (Factors/Raw Scales) for GLM and Tree
# 'test_dummy' (Normalized/Dummified) for KNN, ANN, and SVM
test_raw   <- readRDS("test_original.rds")
test_dummy <- readRDS("test_dummy.rds")

# Ensure target variables are factors
# Check your specific column names if these throw errors!
actual_raw   <- as.factor(test_raw$History.of.Mental.Illness)
actual_dummy <- as.factor(test_dummy$history_of_mental_illness_yes)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 1. LOGISTIC REGRESSION (Using Raw Data)
# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Predict probability, then convert to Yes/No based on 0.5 threshold
prob_glm <- predict(m_glm, test_raw, type = "response")
pred_glm <- factor(ifelse(prob_glm > 0.34, "Yes", "No"), levels = c("No", "Yes"))

cat("\n--- LOGISTIC REGRESSION ---\n")
print(confusionMatrix(pred_glm, actual_raw, positive = "Yes"))

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 2. K-NEAREST NEIGHBORS (Fixing the Dimension Error)
# ---------------------------------------------------------
m_knn <- readRDS("knn_model_object.rds")

# We create a temporary version of the data without the target column
# Replace 'history_of_mental_illness_yes' with whatever your target column name is
test_features_only <- test_dummy[, !names(test_dummy) %in% c("history_of_mental_illness_yes")]

# Now predict using the features only
pred_knn <- predict(m_knn, test_features_only, type = "class")

cat("\n--- K-NEAREST NEIGHBORS ---\n")
print(confusionMatrix(pred_knn, actual_dummy, positive = "1"))

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 3. ARTIFICIAL NEURAL NETWORK (Using Normalized Data)
# ---------------------------------------------------------
# nnet returns a single numeric column; convert to 0/1 at 0.5
prob_ann <- predict(m_ann, test_dummy, type = "raw")
pred_ann <- factor(ifelse(prob_ann > 0.34, "1", "0"), levels = c("0", "1"))

cat("\n--- NEURAL NETWORK ---\n")
print(confusionMatrix(pred_ann, actual_dummy, positive = "1"))

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 4. SUPPORT VECTOR MACHINE (Custom Threshold: 0.3)
# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Get raw probabilities instead of class labels
# This returns a matrix with columns for "0" and "1"
prob_svm <- predict(m_svm, test_dummy, type = "probabilities")

# 2. Extract the probability for the positive class ("1")
prob_pos_svm <- prob_svm[, "1"]

# 3. Apply your custom threshold of 0.34
pred_svm_34 <- factor(ifelse(prob_pos_svm > 0.3, "1", "0"), levels = c("0", "1"))

cat("\n--- SUPPORT VECTOR MACHINE (Threshold: 0.3) ---\n")
print(confusionMatrix(pred_svm_34, actual_dummy, positive = "1"))

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 5. C5.0 DECISION TREE (Custom Threshold: 0.34)
# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Get probabilities instead of the final class
# We use 'test_raw' because C5.0 was trained on factors/raw scales
prob_tree <- predict(m_tree, test_raw, type = "prob")

# 2. Extract the "Yes" probability column
# If your model levels are 1/0, change "Yes" to "1"
prob_yes_tree <- prob_tree[, "Yes"]

# 3. Apply the 0.34 threshold
# If the probability is > 0.34, label it "Yes", otherwise "No"
pred_tree_34 <- factor(ifelse(prob_yes_tree > 0.5, "Yes", "No"), levels = c("No", "Yes"))

cat("\n--- C5.0 DECISION TREE (Threshold: 0.34) ---\n")
# 4. Generate the Confusion Matrix
print(confusionMatrix(pred_tree_34, actual_raw, positive = "Yes"))

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 6. RANDOM FOREST EXPERT (Using Raw Data)
# ---------------------------------------------------------
m_rf_expert <- readRDS("rf_model.rds")

# Generate probabilities
prob_rf_expert <- predict(m_rf_expert, test_raw, type = "prob")

# Apply standard 0.5 threshold or your preferred custom threshold
pred_rf_expert <- factor(ifelse(prob_rf_expert[, "Yes"] > 0.34, "Yes", "No"), 
                         levels = c("No", "Yes"))

cat("\n--- RANDOM FOREST EXPERT ---\n")
cm_rf_expert <- confusionMatrix(pred_rf_expert, actual_raw, positive = "Yes")
print(cm_rf_expert)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# MASTER PERFORMANCE SUMMARY TABLE (Sorted by Sensitivity)
# ---------------------------------------------------------
library(knitr)
library(dplyr)
library(randomForest) 

# 1. Helper function to extract metrics with a 'Type' tag
extract_metrics <- function(cm, model_name, type) {
  data.frame(
    Category = type,
    Model = model_name,
    Accuracy = round(as.numeric(cm$overall["Accuracy"]), 4),
    Kappa = round(as.numeric(cm$overall["Kappa"]), 4),
    Sensitivity = round(as.numeric(cm$byClass["Sensitivity"]), 4),
    Specificity = round(as.numeric(cm$byClass["Specificity"]), 4),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

# 2. Combine all models into one data frame
# Note: Ensure these CM objects are named correctly based on your previous chunks
performance_report <- rbind(
  # Stacked Level 1 Models
  extract_metrics(cm_tree, "Meta-Tree (C5.0)", "Stacked"),
  extract_metrics(cm_rf, "Meta-Random Forest", "Stacked"),
  
  # Individual Expert Models
  extract_metrics(cm_rf_expert, "Random Forest", "Individual"),
  extract_metrics(confusionMatrix(pred_glm, actual_raw, positive = "Yes"), "Logistic Regression", "Individual"),
  extract_metrics(confusionMatrix(pred_knn, actual_dummy, positive = "1"), "KNN", "Individual"),
  extract_metrics(confusionMatrix(pred_ann, actual_dummy, positive = "1"), "Neural Network", "Individual"),
  extract_metrics(confusionMatrix(pred_svm_34, actual_dummy, positive = "1"), "SVM", "Individual"),
  extract_metrics(confusionMatrix(pred_tree_34, actual_raw, positive = "Yes"), "C5.0 Tree", "Individual")
)

# 3. THE SORT: Order by Sensitivity in descending order (highest to lowest)
performance_report <- performance_report[order(performance_report$Sensitivity, decreasing = TRUE), ]

# 4. Final Formatting: Multiply by 100 for percentage-style reading
performance_report_clean <- performance_report %>%
  mutate(across(c(Accuracy, Kappa, Sensitivity, Specificity), ~ .x * 100)) %>%
  rename(`Sensitivity (Catch Rate %)` = Sensitivity,
         `Accuracy (%)` = Accuracy)

# 5. Display the Table
kable(performance_report_clean, 
      caption = "414-Insurance: Model Leaderboard (Ordered by Sensitivity)",
      align = "llcccc")

# Save final results for your report
saveRDS(performance_report_clean, "final_model_comparison_table.rds")
```
